updated: July 24, 2026. CPR invoice is paid, Kathleen in finance marked it Processed this morning, so that is cleared ahead of the July 27 due date. The pay card picture has not moved: yesterday, Thursday July 23, was Dawn's scheduled pickup, and Je'Tina told Jordan-Kallel's father that evening the cards still are not in and she expects them next week. So either the July 23 pickup slipped or it was missed. Confirm which with Dawn. Keosha still needs her new time and the topics answer, and Ashleigh still needs a day or a no.
headline: Week 3 wrapping · CPR invoice paid, cleared before the Jul 27 due date · Pay cards still not in, yesterday's pickup slot passed and Je'Tina's word is next week · Keosha still needs her new time · Ashleigh needs a day or a no

week1.tag: No power at the Branch after Friday's storm
week1.note: Canceled. Orientation moves to Wednesday. Everyone still gets paid for this day. Families were notified the night of July 6.

week1.tag: Done. Orientation plus U.S. DOL, in person 10 to 2
week1.note: Done. This was the first day. Orientation with Jermaine and Je'Tina, the U.S. Department of Labor (Mildred Kress) on wages and workplace rights, then AI Day Part 1 and Part 2.

sessions.activity: AI Day Part 1, What AI Really Is
AI Day Part 2, Resume From Zero

week1.tag: Done. Workplace Foundations · in person, 10 to 2
week1.note: Done. Day two, in person 10 to 2. Team-building and AI reflection, a built work-readiness session, then Workplace Ethics Court and Time Management.

sessions.activity: Slot a built session (Interview / Job Post / Decode)

week1.note: Done. Virtual and self-paced. Youth submitted their Capstone Kickoff on their own time. This closed out Week 1.

doNow.title: Replies sent to Wayne State and DTE
doNow.desc: Alex at WSU has the logistics (no bus, Welcome Center drop off, walk to the W) and the Student Success ask. Jon at DTE is confirmed for Wed 7/29 at noon.

doNow.title: Find Ashleigh a new day, or let it go
doNow.desc: She cannot make Thursday 7/23 and wants another day. Week 4 is tight: Tuesday is Wayne State all day, Wednesday has Keosha at 11 and DTE at noon, Thursday has CPR from 11 to 1. Realistic options are Thu 7/30 at 1:00, or Week 5 which you are away for. Your call whether to chase it.

doNow.title: Tell Keosha her time moved to 11:00
doNow.desc: You had her in writing for 12 noon on Wed 7/29. DTE took that hour, so she moves to 11:00 same day. She is also waiting on two answers: what health topics the youth want, and whether any of them have asthma.

doNow.title: Send Jon the calendar invite
doNow.desc: He asked for it directly. Wed 7/29, 12:00 to 1:00, the Branch, 8220 Second Avenue.

doNow.title: Payroll 2 Transcript submitted
doNow.desc: Sent July 20 at 2:23 PM to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org, cc Gwendolyn and Je'Tina, in before the 5 PM deadline. GDYT replied Received on July 21, so the transcript is confirmed in.

doNow.title: Settle the pay card story, the Branch and GDYT are saying two different things
doNow.desc: Je'Tina answered Jordan-Kallel's father the evening of July 23 and told him the cards have not come in yet, that she expects pickup sometime next week, and that each participant will sign for their own card and take it home. That is not what Dawn sent. Dawn set the pickup for Thursday July 23, 9:30 to 5, at Connect Detroit, 613 Abbott Street, Amphitheatre on the lower level, by Calendly appointment, an agency rep picks up all 15 cards, bring documentation of time worked, youth acknowledge receipt by QR code or Jotform, and cards cannot be mailed. That day has now passed. So either the July 23 pickup slipped or it was missed. Confirm which with Dawn, then get one answer to the families. If yesterday's slot was missed, call Dawn this morning. First pay is July 31.

doNow.title: Wayne State answered
doNow.desc: You replied to Alex this morning, July 23 at 10:25, copying Jessica Archer and Je'Tina. The schedule stands: Old Main museums, Dr. Chike, campus tour. You told him there is no bus, parents drop at the Welcome Center, we walk down to the W, and he brings them back to the Welcome Center at the end. You also asked for someone from Admission and Student Success to speak at lunch. Now it is on Alex to confirm the Student Success speaker and what happens for lunch itself.

doNow.title: YPC registration confirmed
doNow.desc: Danielle Boone at Connect Detroit confirmed July 22 that the registration came through. Je'Tina submitted the form for 3 adults and 16 participants. Danielle was clear that they go by the form only, they did not compare it against the attendee list Je'Tina emailed, and they cannot add anyone who was not on the submission. So the head count is fixed at what went in. The workshop half was already closed out, Jermaine passed on presenting because he is out of the country August 4 to 11 and Shuna accepted that July 17.

doNow.title: Cover the banking side of Week 2 (Huntington out)
doNow.desc: Courtney Elledge at Huntington wrote back and cannot do Banking 101 this year. She offered to send financial education and banking materials for the youth to use during the week. So now both Banking 101 and the credit session still need a plan. Take her materials, and either find a fast replacement or have Jermaine run Banking 101. Week 2 is already underway, so this needs a plan now.

doNow.title: CPR invoice paid
doNow.desc: Done. Natosha Gurley at Rhythm of Life sent invoice #M4ITOVW8-0001 on July 20, 16 youth at $12 each ($192) and 5 staff or youth council at the AHA Friends and Family rate of $50 each ($250), $442 total, due July 27. Kamilia approved it July 21 and Kathleen Coney in finance marked it Processed on July 24, cleared ahead of the due date.

doNow.title: Confirm all 15 are on the final GDYT approved list
doNow.desc: Nicole Hill's July 14 update says the Youth Enrollment Tracker now holds the final Approved for Placement list, and only youth on it can work or be paid. Youth who worked before being approved cannot be paid for that time, and GDYT will not cover unapproved youth. Check the YET and confirm all 15 of ours show Approved for Placement. Also get the worksite ready for GDYT monitor visits over the next few weeks: original work permits on hand and a first aid kit.

doNow.title: Send Connect Detroit the payroll backup docs
doNow.desc: Now that the transcript is in, they need your proof: signed daily sign-in sheets and benchmark tracking. Remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has it.

doNow.title: Get the 15 youth into the GDYT Empower App
doNow.desc: Kelsey at Connect Detroit sent the app sign-up reminder with a youth instructions deck. The app's modules also satisfy the GDYT financial literacy requirement, and it's separate from payroll. The GDYT App team now sends an automatic weekly module progress report, the first arrived July 20 at 6 AM listing which youth have signed up and their module counts. Anyone not on the report has not signed up yet. Sign up group code is naacp-1024 at empowerme.app/gdyt, so use the report to chase whoever is still missing.

doNow.title: Lock the rest of Week 4
doNow.desc: CPR is locked for Thursday July 30 (approved) and DTE is locked for Wednesday July 29 at noon. Still need Keosha's health day and Zipporah's Financial Wellness date. Noon on July 29 is now DTE's, so Keosha goes to July 30 or to a different hour on the 29th. She wrote back July 22 asking what health topics the young people want covered and whether any of them has asthma, so answer that when you give her the new time.

doNow.title: Jon Johnson has the details and a day-of contact
doNow.desc: You wrote him back this morning, July 23 at 10:27, with the date, the group, and a name and number for the day of. Mark Jones, Monique Johnson, Je'Tina and Kamilia are all copied. Monique is out of the office until August 4. The one piece still owed is the calendar invite itself.

doNow.title: Two Week 3 virtual requests still open
doNow.desc: Je'Tina answered Jordan-Kallel's father tonight and told him there are no virtual assignments this week and we will welcome his son back when he returns. That closes his ask, so decide how his missed days count for benchmarks and pay. Still open: the brother and sister who emailed July 12 asking to work virtually July 20 to 29, fully out July 21 to 24. That window covered the in-person heart of Week 3, the Dream Car Reveal Tue 7/21 and the Entrepreneur Panel Thu 7/23. Give those two a plan so they keep hours and stay on the Capstone.

doNow.title: Confirm the two bounced families are set for the rest
doNow.desc: Two addresses bounced on the July 6 all-family email. Week 1 is done. Confirm both families by text or call that they have the schedule for the remaining weeks. One of the two also has a working gmail on the same thread.

items.who: NAACP staff, Tue 8/4. Jermaine is out of the country Aug 4 to 11, so staff run the hotline.

items.who: Jon Johnson asked for Tue 8/4, the Election Primary. Jermaine offered Wed 7/22 or Wed 7/29 instead and Jon took 7/29. DTE is a Week 4 item now.

items.who: Darryl Woods, Thu 8/6, 11a (text to send). Jermaine is out Aug 4 to 11, so line up who hosts the room.

waiting.name: Wayne State campus day (Jessica Archer, Alex Azzopardi)
waiting.note: Answered July 23 at 10:25. Alex had sent the tentative July 28 run of the day on July 22: welcome at the W sign on Warren, Old Main museums, a lecture with Dr. Chike in 2120 State Hall, a campus tour, out at 2:00. You told him there is no bus, parents drop at the Welcome Center, we walk down to the W, and he returns them to the Welcome Center at the end, and you asked for an Admission and Student Success speaker at lunch. Waiting on him to confirm that speaker and what the group does for lunch.

waiting.name: Zipporah Bolar
waiting.note: Checking her schedule for the Financial Wellness session (Week 2 or 4).

waiting.name: GDYT payroll + pay cards
waiting.note: Payroll 2 pivoted again July 17. myOneFlow will not be ready, so no one touches the system. Each partner fills out one customized Payroll Transcript, the Excel form Connect Detroit sends, and emails it to gdytpartnermessage@ConnectDetroit.org by 5:00 PM Monday July 20. No other format accepted, one per agency. Tab A is total benchmarks completed per youth for July 6 to 10 and July 13 to 17, Tab B is the authorization signature. The file is sorted, the first attachment was named for Mack Alive but Nicole corrected it and the right form is named NAACP Hybrid Payroll #2 Transcript. Backup documentation, your own signed sign in and benchmark sheets, is still required and remaining pay is held until Connect Detroit has it. Only youth on the final Approved for Placement list in the Youth Enrollment Tracker are paid, so confirm all 15 are on it (see Do Now), and GDYT monitors will visit to check safety, supervision, original work permits and a first aid kit. Our group is Payroll 2, first pay July 31. Dawn confirmed the pay card pickup July 20: Thursday July 23, 9:30 to 5, at Connect Detroit, 613 Abbott Street, by Calendly appointment, agency rep only. Bring documentation of time worked, do not send youth, and youth acknowledge receipt by QR code. Cards cannot be mailed to youth. That is where the two stories split: Je'Tina told a parent tonight that no cards have come in, that pickup is likely next week, and that each young person signs for their own card. Get one answer and hold to it (see Do Now). GDYT replied Received on July 21, so the transcript is confirmed in.

waiting.name: GDYT Empower App
waiting.note: Kelsey (Connect Detroit) sent app sign-up instructions Jul 6, with a youth how-to deck attached. Youth register in the Empower App; its modules cover the GDYT financial literacy requirement (separate from payroll). The GDYT App team now sends an automatic weekly module progress report, the first arrived July 20 at 6 AM with per youth module counts, and anyone missing from it has not signed up yet. Sign up group code naacp-1024 at empowerme.app/gdyt.

waiting.name: A participant asked for Wednesdays off
waiting.note: One of the young people emailed July 14 asking for Wednesdays off starting the week of July 22 to cover his other job. He works this full week out. Plan Wednesday coverage for the coming weeks without him. He is a participant, not staff.

needs.name: Shantael Harbin
needs.note: SEL confirmed, invoice sent. Pay the $1,500.

needs.name: Two participants, Week 3 virtual
needs.note: A brother and sister emailed July 12, parent copied, asking to go virtual July 20 to 29, fully out July 21 to 24. That is the in-person core of Week 3. Set a plan so they keep hours and stay on the Capstone. Jordan-Kallel's piece is answered, Je'Tina told his father July 23 there are no virtual assignments this week and he comes back when he returns.

needs.name: Keosha King
needs.note: Wrote back July 22 asking what health topics the young people are interested in and whether any of them has asthma. She is also waiting on a new time, her noon slot on July 29 went to DTE. Send her both, the new hour and the topics.

closed.name: Jon Johnson (DTE)
closed.note: Settled July 22 and confirmed back July 23 at 10:27. Jon took Wednesday July 29 at 12:00, in person at the Branch. August 4 was off the table because it is the Election Primary, and 7/22 was the other option he passed on. Your reply gave him the date, the group of 15 ages 14 to 19, and a day of contact. Only the calendar invite is still owed. Mark, Monique, Je'Tina and Kamilia are all on the thread, and Monique is out until August 4.

closed.name: Young Professionals Conference (Connect Detroit)
closed.note: Wednesday August 5 at Huntington Place, 8:30 to 2:00, sponsored by DWIHN for the summer youth employment programs, free. Danielle Boone confirmed July 22 that the registration is received. Je'Tina submitted the form for 3 adults and 16 participants. Danielle said they go strictly by the form, she did not check it against the attendee list Je'Tina emailed, and no one can be added now. The workshop half closed July 17 when Shuna accepted Jermaine's pass, he is out of the country August 4 to 11.

closed.name: Huntington (Courtney Elledge)
closed.note: Replied July 10. Cannot do Banking 101 in person this year, her schedule got too tight. She offered to send financial education and banking materials for the youth to use during the week. Banking 101 now needs a plan (see Do Now).

closed.name: Week 1 logistics sent to families
closed.note: All-family email went out the night of July 6: no Tuesday, start Wednesday July 8, 10 to 2, bring a device, Tuesday pay covered. Two addresses bounced on July 7, follow up with those two families by phone.

closed.name: DTE reply sent

closed.name: Kamilia, Micah, Keosha

items.title: Weeks 1 and 2 are done, Week 3 is running
items.desc: Week 1 opened the program: orientation, the DOL, AI Day, Workplace Foundations, and an async Capstone Kickoff on the Friday. Week 2 was Financial Literacy, July 13 to 17. Week 3, Entrepreneurship and AI for Creators, is in person now and fully locked after DTE moved to July 29.

items.title: Roster locked

items.title: Orientation delivered
items.desc: Deck, family agenda, take-home guide, registration form all out.

items.title: Week 1 curriculum built
items.desc: All 6 sessions (guide + Signature deck), the Day One intake sheet, and a START HERE map. Volunteer-runnable, free ChatGPT. In Curriculum/Week 1.

items.title: Week 2 curriculum built, Life Be Life'ing rebuilt
items.desc: Six sessions with guides and decks. Life Be Life'ing was rebuilt from your 2024 facilitator guide with your roles, scripts and numbers kept word for word, and four new rules marked NEW so you can cut them: how you win, The Floor, the Reality Check and The Choice. Four print pieces in the Signature: Session Guide, Family Budget Sheet, Role Cards, Life Event Cards.
